# Transcrição: 8. Be The First to Follow-Up

- **Fonte:** 8. Be The First to Follow-Up
- **Duração:** 01:15
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:15]

Understand that 35 to 50% of all sales, not conversions, sales, actual money goes to the first vendor or first company to do the first follow-up. Grant, what does that mean, man? You're killing me with these statistics. What I mean is this. You need to start making decisions with your company about when you're running ads. If you're running an ad on Saturday and there's nobody there on Saturday to respond, you are wasting money. Because whosoever responding is going to get the business. 35 to 50% of all sales go to the first person to follow-up, the first company or business or vendor or brand, regardless of price and regardless of quality. Follow-up wins. So you've got to start making decisions like, oh, I don't want to hire any people. People cost money. No, no follow-up costs money. You're better off putting somebody that doesn't know anything about your product, your service, your company, your brand to follow a customer up instantly even if it's only, hey, we saw, I saw you hit our website. I'm not the expert here. I'm going to have somebody call you back on Monday. At least that keeps you in the game. Remember, 35 to 50% of all sales, not conversions, sales go to the first vendor to do the first follow-up.
